seoTitle: SK Hynix Memory Products | DRAM NAND SSD HBM | Authorized Distributor
seoDescription: Browse SK Hynix DRAM, NAND Flash, SSD, and HBM memory products. Technical specifications, selection guide, and application support from authorized distributor.

faqs.question: What memory products does SK Hynix offer?
faqs.answer: SK Hynix offers a comprehensive portfolio of memory and storage solutions: 1) DRAM products including DDR4, DDR5, LPDDR4X, and LPDDR5 for servers, mobile devices, and automotive applications, 2) NAND Flash memory including 3D NAND TLC and QLC for storage applications, 3) SSD solutions including enterprise NVMe SSDs and client SATA SSDs, 4) High Bandwidth Memory (HBM) for AI, HPC, and graphics applications. Each product category is designed to meet specific performance, capacity, and power requirements across data center, mobile, automotive, and consumer markets. As an authorized distributor, we provide the full range of SK Hynix products with technical support and application guidance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select DRAM for system memory, NAND Flash for storage, SSD for high-performance storage, and HBM for AI/HPC applications. Contact our FAE team for product selection assistance.

faqs.question: How do I select the right SK Hynix memory for my application?
faqs.answer: Selecting the right SK Hynix memory requires evaluating several factors: 1) Application type - determine if you need system memory (DRAM), storage (NAND/SSD), or high-bandwidth memory (HBM), 2) Performance requirements - consider bandwidth, latency, and throughput needs, 3) Capacity needs - calculate required memory or storage capacity with growth margin, 4) Power constraints - evaluate power consumption and thermal requirements, 5) Interface compatibility - ensure the memory interface matches your processor or controller, 6) Environmental conditions - consider temperature range and reliability requirements. For servers, DDR4/DDR5 RDIMMs provide high capacity and reliability. For mobile, LPDDR4X/LPDDR5 offers low power consumption. For AI/HPC, HBM provides extreme bandwidth. Our FAE team can help analyze your requirements and recommend optimal SK Hynix solutions.
faqs.decisionGuide: Analyze your performance, capacity, and power requirements. Contact our FAE team for personalized product recommendations based on your specific application.

faqs.question: What are the differences between SK Hynix DRAM generations?
faqs.answer: SK Hynix offers multiple DRAM generations with different characteristics: 1) DDR4 - mature technology with speeds up to 3200MT/s, widely used in current servers and PCs, cost-effective and reliable, 2) DDR5 - next-generation with speeds starting at 4800MT/s, higher bandwidth, improved power efficiency, and enhanced reliability features, ideal for new server designs, 3) LPDDR4X - low-power mobile memory with speeds up to 4266MT/s, optimized for smartphones and tablets, 4) LPDDR5 - latest mobile memory with speeds up to 6400MT/s, significantly lower power consumption than LPDDR4X. DDR5 offers approximately 50% higher bandwidth than DDR4 while maintaining similar power consumption. For new designs, DDR5 provides better performance and future-proofing, while DDR4 remains suitable for cost-sensitive applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use DDR5 for new high-performance designs, DDR4 for cost-sensitive applications. Select LPDDR5 for latest mobile devices, LPDDR4X for existing mobile platforms.

faqs.question: What technical support does BeiLuo provide for SK Hynix products?
faqs.answer: BeiLuo provides comprehensive technical support for SK Hynix products: 1) Product selection - our FAE team helps identify optimal memory solutions based on your requirements, 2) Design support - we provide reference designs, PCB layout guidelines, and signal integrity recommendations, 3) Integration assistance - BIOS/firmware configuration, timing optimization, and system validation support, 4) Troubleshooting - debugging memory issues, compatibility testing, and performance optimization, 5) Quality support - product qualification, reliability testing, and failure analysis assistance, 6) Supply chain - inventory planning, forecast management, and long-term supply agreements. Our technical team has extensive experience across servers, mobile devices, automotive, and industrial applications. Contact our FAE team for personalized support.
faqs.decisionGuide: Engage our FAE team early in your design cycle. Leverage our design review services and reference designs for optimal integration.

faqs.question: What industries does SK Hynix serve with its memory products?
faqs.answer: SK Hynix memory products serve diverse industries worldwide: 1) Data Center and Cloud Computing - high-capacity DRAM and NVMe SSDs for servers and storage infrastructure, 2) Mobile and Consumer Electronics - LPDDR memory for smartphones, tablets, and laptops, 3) Automotive - AEC-Q100 qualified DRAM for ADAS, infotainment, and autonomous driving systems, 4) Artificial Intelligence and HPC - HBM for AI training accelerators and supercomputers, 5) Enterprise Storage - NAND Flash and SSDs for enterprise storage arrays, 6) Industrial and IoT - industrial-grade memory for automation and edge devices. SK Hynix products are found in devices from leading technology companies worldwide, powering everything from smartphones to supercomputers.
faqs.decisionGuide: Identify your industry requirements and select SK Hynix products qualified for your application environment. Contact FAE for industry-specific recommendations.

faqs.question: How does SK Hynix ensure product quality and reliability?
faqs.answer: SK Hynix maintains rigorous quality and reliability standards: 1) Manufacturing excellence - advanced fabrication facilities with strict process control, 2) Testing protocols - comprehensive wafer sort, final test, and system-level validation, 3) Reliability qualification - products undergo accelerated life testing, temperature cycling, and stress testing, 4) Automotive qualification - AEC-Q100 certified products for automotive applications, 5) Quality certifications - ISO 9001, ISO 14001, and IATF 16949 certifications, 6) Failure analysis - advanced FA capabilities to identify and resolve quality issues, 7) Continuous improvement - data-driven quality improvement programs. As an authorized distributor, we provide quality documentation and support for customer qualification processes.
faqs.decisionGuide: Request quality reports and reliability data for your specific application requirements. SK Hynix products meet industry standards for quality and reliability.

categories.name: DRAM
categories.description: High-performance DRAM solutions including DDR4, DDR5, LPDDR4X, and LPDDR5 for servers, mobile devices, and automotive applications
categories.longDescription: SK Hynix DRAM products deliver industry-leading performance and reliability for diverse computing applications. The portfolio includes DDR4 and DDR5 for servers and PCs, LPDDR4X and LPDDR5 for mobile devices, and automotive-grade DRAM for vehicle applications. Key features include high-speed operation with DDR5 reaching 4800MT/s and beyond, low power consumption with LPDDR5 offering significant power savings, wide capacity range from 4GB to 128GB per module, advanced reliability features including on-die ECC, and automotive qualification for harsh environments. As an authorized distributor, we provide comprehensive DRAM selection guidance and technical support.

Select LPDDR5 for latest mobile devices

selectionGuideLink.articleTitle: How to Select SK Hynix DRAM for Your Application
selectionGuideLink.description: Comprehensive guide for selecting SK Hynix DRAM products

faqs.question: What is the difference between DDR4 and DDR5 memory?
faqs.answer: DDR5 represents a significant advancement over DDR4 in several key areas: 1) Bandwidth - DDR5 starts at 4800MT/s compared to DDR4's maximum of 3200MT/s, providing approximately 50% higher bandwidth, 2) Power efficiency - DDR5 operates at 1.1V compared to DDR4's 1.2V, reducing power consumption, 3) Capacity - DDR5 supports higher density chips, enabling larger memory modules up to 128GB, 4) Reliability - DDR5 includes on-die ECC for improved data integrity, 5) Bank architecture - DDR5 uses 32 banks compared to DDR4's 16, improving performance. However, DDR5 requires new motherboard and processor support. For new designs, DDR5 offers better performance and future-proofing, while DDR4 remains suitable for cost-sensitive applications and existing platforms.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose DDR5 for new high-performance server and PC designs. Use DDR4 for cost-sensitive applications or existing platform upgrades.

faqs.question: Does SK Hynix offer automotive-qualified DRAM?
faqs.answer: Yes, SK Hynix offers automotive-grade DRAM products qualified to AEC-Q100 standards: 1) Temperature range - automotive DRAM operates from -40C to +105C or +125C, compared to commercial 0C to +85C, 2) Reliability - enhanced testing and qualification for automotive reliability requirements, 3) Product range - includes DDR4, LPDDR4X, and GDDR6 for various automotive applications, 4) Applications - ADAS systems, infotainment, instrument clusters, and autonomous driving platforms, 5) Longevity - extended product lifecycle support for automotive programs. These products undergo rigorous testing including high-temperature operating life, temperature cycling, and EMC testing.
faqs.decisionGuide: Specify AEC-Q100 qualified SK Hynix DRAM for automotive applications. Contact our automotive team for qualification reports.

faqs.question: What is the difference between RDIMM and UDIMM memory modules?
faqs.answer: RDIMM (Registered DIMM) and UDIMM (Unbuffered DIMM) differ in their target applications and electrical characteristics: 1) Buffering - RDIMM uses a register to buffer address and command signals, reducing electrical load on the memory controller, while UDIMM connects directly without buffering, 2) Capacity support - RDIMM supports higher memory capacities (up to 128GB per module), UDIMM typically limited to 32GB, 3) Applications - RDIMM is standard for servers and workstations, UDIMM for desktop PCs and entry-level servers, 4) Latency - RDIMM has slightly higher latency due to register delay, UDIMM has lower latency, 5) Cost - RDIMM is more expensive due to additional components. For enterprise servers, RDIMM is required for high-capacity configurations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use RDIMM for enterprise servers requiring high capacity. Use UDIMM for desktop PCs and cost-sensitive applications.

faqs.question: How do I calculate the total memory capacity for my server?
faqs.answer: To calculate server memory capacity: 1) Count memory slots - determine available DIMM slots on your motherboard, 2) Check maximum per slot - verify maximum supported capacity per slot (e.g., 64GB, 128GB), 3) Calculate total - multiply slots by maximum per slot, 4) Consider channel population - for optimal performance, populate all channels evenly, 5) Check processor limits - verify your CPU supports the target memory capacity. Example: Server with 16 slots supporting 64GB RDIMMs = 1TB total capacity. For best performance, use identical modules across all channels. Contact FAE for platform-specific capacity planning.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate based on available slots and maximum per-slot capacity. Ensure even channel population for optimal performance.

faqs.question: What is ECC memory and when is it required?
faqs.answer: ECC (Error Correcting Code) memory detects and corrects single-bit errors: 1) Error detection - ECC can detect double-bit errors and correct single-bit errors automatically, 2) Implementation - uses extra memory bits (72-bit vs 64-bit for non-ECC) to store error correction codes, 3) Applications - essential for servers, workstations, and critical systems where data integrity is paramount, 4) Performance impact - minimal performance impact (1-2%) for significant reliability improvement, 5) Cost - ECC memory costs 10-20% more than non-ECC. For mission-critical applications including databases, financial systems, and scientific computing, ECC is strongly recommended.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use ECC for all server and mission-critical applications. Non-ECC is acceptable for consumer desktops and non-critical systems.

products.name: DDR4 32GB RDIMM
products.shortDescription: SK Hynix DDR4 32GB RDIMM 3200MT/s ECC registered memory module for server applications
descriptionParagraphs: The HMAA4GS7AJR8N-XN is a high-capacity DDR4 RDIMM designed for enterprise server and data center applications. This 32GB module operates at 3200MT/s, providing high bandwidth for memory-intensive workloads. The registered design reduces electrical load on the memory controller, enabling high-density memory configurations.

Featuring ECC (Error Correcting Code) support, this module provides data integrity essential for mission-critical server applications. The 2Rx4 rank configuration optimizes performance for dual-processor server platforms. Built with SK Hynix's advanced DRAM technology, it delivers reliable operation under demanding conditions.

This RDIMM is compatible with major server platforms from Intel and AMD. It supports Intel Xeon Scalable processors and AMD EPYC processors, making it ideal for cloud computing, virtualization, and database applications.
features: Compatible with Intel Xeon and AMD EPYC

faeReview.author: Michael Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Server Memory
faeReview.content: The HMAA4GS7AJR8N-XN is an excellent choice for modern server deployments requiring high capacity and reliability. This 32GB RDIMM provides the perfect balance of performance and density. The 3200MT/s speed delivers excellent bandwidth for virtualization and database workloads. I particularly appreciate the ECC implementation which provides robust error correction for mission-critical applications. The module's compatibility with both Intel and AMD platforms gives customers flexibility in server design. For customers building high-capacity servers, these RDIMMs allow up to 2TB per processor on modern platforms. The reliability has been excellent in production deployments. I recommend this module for any enterprise server application requiring high-capacity DDR4 memory.
faeReview.highlight: High-capacity 32GB RDIMM with excellent reliability for enterprise servers

alternativeParts.useCase: Alternative when Micron supply is constrained

faqs.question: What server platforms are compatible with this DDR4 RDIMM?
faqs.answer: The HMAA4GS7AJR8N-XN DDR4 RDIMM is compatible with major server platforms: 1) Intel Xeon Scalable processors (1st through 4th generation) with DDR4 support, 2) AMD EPYC 7002 and 7003 series processors, 3) Compatible with server motherboards from Dell, HPE, Lenovo, Supermicro, and other major vendors, 4) Supports both single-processor and dual-processor configurations. The module follows JEDEC DDR4 standards ensuring broad compatibility.
faqs.decisionGuide: Verify compatibility with your server platform using the manufacturer's memory compatibility list. Contact FAE for platform-specific guidance.

faqs.question: How does ECC work on this memory module?
faqs.answer: The HMAA4GS7AJR8N-XN implements SECDED (Single Error Correction, Double Error Detection) ECC: 1) Error correction - automatically corrects single-bit errors in real-time without system interruption, 2) Error detection - detects and reports double-bit errors that cannot be corrected, 3) Implementation - uses 4 extra bits per 32-bit data word (72-bit total vs 64-bit for non-ECC), 4) Memory controller - server processors have integrated ECC memory controllers that manage error correction, 5) Error logging - corrected errors are logged by the system for monitoring. ECC is essential for server reliability, preventing data corruption from cosmic rays, electrical noise, or other sources.
faqs.decisionGuide: ECC is essential for all server applications. This module provides robust error correction for mission-critical workloads.

faqs.question: What is the maximum memory capacity per server with this RDIMM?
faqs.answer: The maximum memory capacity depends on your server platform: 1) Intel Xeon Scalable - up to 2TB per processor with 16 slots, 2) AMD EPYC - up to 2TB per processor with 8 channels, 3) 2-socket servers - up to 4TB total with full population, 4) Population rules - all channels should be populated evenly for optimal performance, 5) Rank limitations - check your platform's rank limitations per channel. This 32GB RDIMM uses 2Rx4 organization. Most servers support up to 2 ranks per channel. Contact FAE for platform-specific capacity planning.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate based on available slots and platform limitations. Ensure even channel population for best performance.

faqs.question: How do I verify compatibility with my server?
faqs.answer: To verify compatibility: 1) Check server manual - consult your server manufacturer's memory compatibility guide, 2) Use online tools - many vendors provide memory configurators (Dell, HPE, Lenovo), 3) Verify specifications - ensure speed (3200MT/s), type (DDR4 RDIMM), and rank (2Rx4) match, 4) Check BIOS version - update to latest BIOS for best compatibility, 5) Test before deployment - validate in your specific server model. SK Hynix RDIMMs follow JEDEC standards ensuring broad compatibility. Contact FAE if you encounter compatibility issues.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use vendor configurators and verify specifications. Test in target server before large deployment.

faqs.question: What is the difference between 2Rx4 and 2Rx8 RDIMMs?
faqs.answer: 2Rx4 and 2Rx8 refer to rank and bit organization: 1) Rank - both are dual-rank (2R), appearing as two memory banks to the system, 2) Bit width - x4 means 4-bit wide data path per chip, x8 means 8-bit wide, 3) Chip count - x4 uses more chips than x8 for same capacity, 4) Performance - x4 can offer better performance through higher parallelism, 5) Reliability - x4 allows finer granularity for error correction, 6) Compatibility - some servers prefer x4 for higher capacity. This module uses 2Rx4 for optimal server performance. Check your server documentation for rank requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: 2Rx4 is preferred for servers. Verify your platform supports the rank configuration before ordering.

products.name: DDR5 64GB RDIMM
products.shortDescription: SK Hynix DDR5 64GB RDIMM 4800MT/s ECC registered memory module for next-generation servers
descriptionParagraphs: The HMCG88MEBRA115N is a high-capacity DDR5 RDIMM designed for next-generation enterprise servers and data centers. This 64GB module operates at 4800MT/s, delivering significantly higher bandwidth than DDR4 for memory-intensive workloads. The registered design supports high-density memory configurations in modern server platforms.

Featuring on-die ECC and advanced reliability features, this module provides exceptional data integrity for mission-critical applications. The DDR5 architecture includes improvements in power efficiency with 1.1V operation, reducing overall system power consumption. The module supports Intel Xeon Scalable and AMD EPYC platforms with DDR5 support.

With 50% higher bandwidth than comparable DDR4 modules, this DDR5 RDIMM enables better performance for virtualization, databases, and AI workloads. The 64GB capacity supports high-density server configurations, allowing up to 4TB per processor on modern platforms.
features: Supports next-gen Intel and AMD platforms

faeReview.author: Sarah Johnson
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Advanced Memory
faeReview.content: The HMCG88MEBRA115N represents the cutting edge of server memory technology. I'm impressed by the performance gains this module delivers. The 4800MT/s speed provides noticeable improvements in memory-bound applications compared to DDR4. The 64GB capacity is perfect for high-density servers where memory capacity per slot is critical. I particularly like the on-die ECC feature which enhances reliability without system-level overhead. The power efficiency at 1.1V is excellent, helping reduce data center power consumption. For customers building next-generation servers, this DDR5 module is essential. The performance uplift is especially noticeable in virtualization and database workloads.
faeReview.highlight: Next-generation DDR5 with exceptional capacity and bandwidth for modern data centers

alternativeParts.reason: Better power efficiency at equivalent performance

faqs.question: What platforms support this DDR5 RDIMM?
faqs.answer: The HMCG88MEBRA115N DDR5 RDIMM supports next-generation server platforms: 1) Intel Xeon Scalable 4th generation (Sapphire Rapids) and newer with DDR5 support, 2) AMD EPYC 9004 series (Genoa) and newer processors, 3) Compatible with DDR5-enabled server motherboards from major vendors, 4) Requires platforms specifically designed for DDR5 memory. DDR5 is not backward compatible with DDR4 platforms.
faqs.decisionGuide: Verify your platform supports DDR5 before ordering. Contact FAE for DDR5 migration planning and platform recommendations.

faqs.question: How much performance improvement does DDR5 provide over DDR4?
faqs.answer: DDR5 provides significant performance improvements over DDR4: 1) Bandwidth - DDR5 4800MT/s offers approximately 50% higher bandwidth than DDR4 3200MT/s, 2) Latency - comparable or slightly improved latency despite higher speed, 3) Real-world performance - 15-30% improvement in memory-intensive applications, 4) Power efficiency - 1.1V vs 1.2V reduces power consumption by approximately 10-15%, 5) Capacity - supports higher density enabling larger memory configurations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Expect 15-30% performance improvement for memory-intensive workloads. DDR5 is essential for next-generation server designs.

faqs.question: What is on-die ECC and how does it differ from standard ECC?
faqs.answer: DDR5 on-die ECC is different from traditional ECC: 1) On-die ECC - corrects errors within the DRAM chip itself before data leaves the die, 2) Standard ECC - uses extra DRAM chip to store error correction codes across 64-bit words, 3) Benefits - on-die ECC improves reliability without system-level ECC overhead, 4) Combined use - DDR5 RDIMMs include both on-die ECC and standard ECC for maximum protection, 5) Error coverage - on-die ECC handles single-bit errors within chips, standard ECC handles multi-bit errors across the interface. This dual-layer protection makes DDR5 ideal for mission-critical applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: DDR5 provides enhanced reliability through on-die ECC combined with standard ECC. Ideal for critical server applications.

faqs.question: How do I migrate from DDR4 to DDR5 in my data center?
faqs.answer: DDR5 migration requires platform refresh: 1) New processors required - DDR5 is not backward compatible with DDR4 platforms, 2) Intel platforms - 4th Gen Xeon Scalable (Sapphire Rapids) and newer support DDR5, 3) AMD platforms - EPYC 9004 series (Genoa) and newer support DDR5, 4) Server refresh - plan server replacement or new deployments for DDR5 adoption, 5) Performance validation - benchmark critical workloads on DDR5 platforms before migration. DDR5 provides 50% bandwidth improvement justifying the migration investment. Plan migration during normal hardware refresh cycles.
faqs.decisionGuide: DDR5 requires new server platforms. Plan adoption during hardware refresh cycles for optimal ROI.

faqs.question: What thermal considerations apply to high-capacity DDR5 RDIMMs?
faqs.answer: High-capacity DDR5 RDIMMs require thermal attention: 1) Power consumption - 64GB DDR5 RDIMM consumes approximately 5-7W under load, 2) Heat density - high capacity in standard form factor concentrates heat, 3) Server cooling - ensure adequate airflow through memory slots, 4) Temperature monitoring - use server management tools to monitor DIMM temperatures, 5) Throttling - DDR5 includes thermal throttling to prevent overheating, 6) Ambient temperature - maintain recommended data center temperatures (18-27C). Proper thermal management ensures reliable operation and maximum performance. Contact FAE for thermal design guidance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Ensure adequate server cooling for high-capacity DDR5. Monitor temperatures and maintain recommended ambient conditions.

categories.name: NAND Flash
categories.description: Advanced 3D NAND Flash memory solutions with high density and reliability for storage applications
categories.longDescription: SK Hynix NAND Flash products deliver high-density, reliable storage solutions for diverse applications. The 3D NAND technology enables vertical stacking of memory cells, achieving higher densities and better performance than planar NAND. The portfolio includes TLC (Triple-Level Cell) for balanced performance and cost, and QLC (Quad-Level Cell) for high-capacity read-intensive applications. Key features include high densities from 128Gb to 1Tb per die, fast interface speeds supporting Toggle and ONFI protocols, robust error correction and data retention, wide operating temperature ranges, and multiple package options for flexible integration. As an authorized distributor, we provide NAND Flash selection guidance and technical support.

series.name: 3D TLC NAND
series.description: Triple-Level Cell NAND with optimal balance of performance, endurance, and cost

series.name: 3D QLC NAND
series.description: Quad-Level Cell NAND for highest density and read-intensive applications

selectionGuide.title: SK Hynix NAND Flash Selection Guide
selectionGuide.content: Guide for selecting optimal NAND Flash based on application requirements
recommendations: Use TLC NAND for balanced performance and endurance
Select QLC NAND for high-capacity read-intensive storage

selectionGuideLink.articleTitle: How to Select SK Hynix NAND Flash for Your Application
selectionGuideLink.description: Comprehensive guide for selecting SK Hynix NAND Flash products

faqs.question: What is the difference between TLC and QLC NAND Flash?
faqs.answer: TLC (Triple-Level Cell) and QLC (Quad-Level Cell) differ in bits stored per cell and characteristics: 1) Bits per cell - TLC stores 3 bits per cell, QLC stores 4 bits per cell, 2) Density - QLC provides 33% higher density than TLC, enabling lower cost per gigabyte, 3) Performance - TLC offers better write performance and lower latency than QLC, 4) Endurance - TLC typically supports 1,000-3,000 P/E cycles, QLC supports 100-1,000 cycles, 5) Applications - TLC is ideal for general-purpose SSDs and mixed workloads, QLC is best for read-intensive applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use TLC NAND for mixed workloads and better endurance. Select QLC NAND for read-intensive, high-capacity storage applications.

faqs.question: What is 3D NAND and what are its advantages?
faqs.answer: 3D NAND is a flash memory technology that stacks memory cells vertically in multiple layers: 1) Higher density - vertical stacking enables much higher densities than planar NAND, 2) Better performance - shorter cell-to-cell distances improve read/write speeds, 3) Improved reliability - larger cell sizes in 3D NAND provide better data retention and endurance, 4) Lower power consumption - more efficient cell structure reduces operating power, 5) Cost efficiency - higher density per wafer reduces manufacturing cost. SK Hynix 3D NAND uses advanced architectures with over 100 layers, achieving densities up to 1Tb per die.
faqs.decisionGuide: 3D NAND is the standard for modern storage. Use SK Hynix 3D NAND for high-density, reliable storage solutions.

faqs.question: What interface options are available for SK Hynix NAND Flash?
faqs.answer: SK Hynix NAND Flash supports multiple interface options: 1) Toggle DDR - high-speed interface developed by Samsung and SK Hynix, supports data rates up to 1200MT/s, 2) ONFI (Open NAND Flash Interface) - industry standard interface supported by most NAND manufacturers, 3) SPI NAND - serial interface for low-pin-count embedded applications, 4) NV-DDR - non-volatile DDR interface for high-performance SSDs. Toggle DDR is the most common interface for high-performance applications. ONFI provides broader compatibility across vendors. SPI NAND is ideal for space-constrained embedded systems. Select interface based on your controller compatibility and performance requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use Toggle DDR for high-performance SSDs. Use ONFI for broad controller compatibility. Use SPI NAND for embedded applications.

faqs.question: How do I calculate NAND Flash endurance for my application?
faqs.answer: To calculate NAND Flash endurance requirements: 1) Determine daily write volume - estimate GB written per day by your application, 2) Calculate required lifetime - determine product lifespan in years, 3) Compute total writes - daily writes × 365 × years, 4) Add safety margin - multiply by 1.5-2x for headroom, 5) Select NAND type - TLC for 1,000-3,000 P/E cycles, QLC for 100-1,000 cycles. Example: 100GB/day × 365 days × 5 years = 182.5TB total. For a 1TB SSD, this equals 182.5 P/E cycles. TLC NAND with 1,000 cycles provides adequate margin. Contact FAE for detailed endurance analysis.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate total writes over product lifetime and select NAND type with adequate P/E cycles. Include 50-100% safety margin.

faqs.question: What is bad block management in NAND Flash?
faqs.answer: Bad block management is essential for NAND Flash reliability: 1) Factory bad blocks - some blocks are marked bad during manufacturing and must not be used, 2) Wear-induced bad blocks - blocks that fail after extensive program/erase cycles, 3) Management strategies - maintain bad block table, implement wear leveling, reserve spare blocks, 4) ECC requirements - strong error correction for marginal blocks, 5) SSD controller role - modern controllers handle bad block management automatically. Proper bad block management extends NAND lifetime and maintains data integrity. SK Hynix NAND includes initial bad block information and supports standard management protocols.
faqs.decisionGuide: Ensure your controller implements proper bad block management. Reserve 2-5% spare blocks for replacement.

products.name: 3D TLC NAND 512Gb
products.shortDescription: SK Hynix 3D TLC NAND Flash 512Gb high-density storage solution with Toggle DDR interface
descriptionParagraphs: The H27QCG8D2M5R is a high-density 3D TLC NAND Flash device offering 512Gb (64GB) storage capacity. This advanced NAND utilizes SK Hynix's 3D stacking technology with over 100 layers, delivering high density and excellent reliability. The Toggle DDR interface supports fast data transfer rates up to 800MT/s.

Designed for SSDs and embedded storage applications, this TLC NAND provides an optimal balance of performance, endurance, and cost. The device supports 1,000-3,000 P/E cycles, making it suitable for consumer and enterprise SSDs. Advanced error correction and bad block management features ensure data integrity.

The BGA-152 package enables compact designs while maintaining excellent thermal performance. This NAND is ideal for SATA and NVMe SSDs, mobile storage, and industrial applications requiring high-capacity, reliable storage.

faeReview.author: David Kim
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Storage Solutions
faeReview.content: The H27QCG8D2M5R is an excellent TLC NAND choice for mainstream SSD applications. This 512Gb device provides the right balance of density, performance, and cost. The Toggle DDR interface delivers consistent high-speed performance for both sequential and random workloads. I particularly appreciate the reliability characteristics - the 3D architecture provides better data retention than older planar NAND. For SSD designers, this NAND works well with popular controllers and supports advanced features like LDPC error correction. The BGA-152 package is easy to work with for both prototyping and production. I've seen this NAND used successfully in consumer SSDs, industrial storage, and even some entry-level enterprise applications.
faeReview.highlight: High-density TLC NAND with excellent balance of performance and reliability

companionParts.description: Phison E12 NVMe SSD controller compatible with this NAND

faqs.question: What is the typical endurance of this TLC NAND?
faqs.answer: The H27QCG8D2M5R TLC NAND provides endurance of 1,000-3,000 P/E (Program/Erase) cycles: 1) Consumer grade - typically 1,000 P/E cycles, suitable for client SSDs with moderate write workloads, 2) Industrial grade - up to 3,000 P/E cycles for more demanding applications, 3) Endurance varies with operating conditions - lower temperatures and optimal voltage extend life, 4) Write amplification - actual endurance depends on write amplification factor in the SSD controller, 5) Over-provisioning - additional spare blocks extend effective endurance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate TBW requirements based on write workload. Contact FAE for endurance analysis and over-provisioning recommendations.

faqs.question: What SSD controllers work with this NAND?
faqs.answer: The H27QCG8D2M5R NAND is compatible with popular SSD controllers: 1) Phison controllers - E12, E13T, S12T series, 2) SMI controllers - SM2262EN, SM2263XT, 3) Marvell controllers - 88SS1074, 88SS1084, 4) InnoGrit controllers - IG5236, IG5666. The Toggle DDR 4.0 interface is widely supported. When selecting a controller, consider: target performance (sequential read/write speeds), interface (SATA vs NVMe), LDPC ECC strength, and DRAM vs DRAMless implementation. Our FAE team can provide controller compatibility recommendations for your specific application requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select controllers based on target performance and interface requirements. Contact FAE for controller compatibility guidance.

faqs.question: What is the page and block size of this NAND?
faqs.answer: The H27QCG8D2M5R features: 1) Page size - 16KB per page for efficient data transfer, 2) Block size - 4MB per block containing multiple pages, 3) Page structure - each page includes data area and spare area for ECC, 4) Block architecture - blocks are the minimum erase unit in NAND Flash, 5) Performance - large page size enables high sequential throughput. Understanding page and block structure is essential for optimizing SSD firmware and maximizing performance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Design firmware to optimize for 16KB page size. Plan erase operations at 4MB block granularity.

faqs.question: What is the data retention specification?
faqs.answer: Data retention specifications for H27QCG8D2M5R: 1) Standard retention - 10 years for fresh data (low P/E cycles), 2) End-of-life retention - 1 year at maximum rated P/E cycles, 3) Temperature dependence - retention decreases at higher temperatures, 4) Read disturb - excessive reads can affect adjacent cells, 5) Storage conditions - store unprogrammed devices in controlled environment. Data retention is critical for archival applications. Implement periodic refresh for long-term storage applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Plan for data refresh in long-term storage. Monitor retention characteristics as NAND ages.

faqs.question: What package options are available?
faqs.answer: The H27QCG8D2M5R is available in BGA-152 package: 1) Package type - BGA (Ball Grid Array) with 152 balls, 2) Dimensions - compact footprint suitable for space-constrained designs, 3) Ball pitch - standard pitch for automated assembly, 4) Thermal characteristics - designed for proper heat dissipation, 5) PCB requirements - follow SK Hynix layout guidelines for optimal signal integrity. BGA packages require proper PCB design and assembly processes. Contact FAE for package drawings and layout recommendations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use BGA-152 for standard SSD designs. Follow SK Hynix PCB layout guidelines.

products.name: 3D TLC NAND 1Tb
products.shortDescription: SK Hynix 3D TLC NAND Flash 1Tb high-capacity storage solution with advanced 3D architecture
descriptionParagraphs: The H27TDG8T2D5R is a high-capacity 3D TLC NAND Flash device offering 1Tb (128GB) storage capacity. This advanced NAND uses SK Hynix's latest 3D stacking technology with over 100 layers, delivering the highest density in a single die. The Toggle DDR interface supports data transfer rates up to 1200MT/s for high-performance applications.

Designed for enterprise and consumer SSDs, this high-capacity NAND provides excellent value for storage-intensive applications. The device supports 1,000-3,000 P/E cycles ensuring long product life. Advanced error correction including LDPC provides robust data protection.

The BGA-272 package accommodates the higher pin count required for 1Tb density while maintaining excellent thermal characteristics. This NAND is ideal for high-capacity NVMe SSDs, data center storage, and enterprise storage solutions.

faeReview.author: Lisa Wang
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Enterprise Storage
faeReview.content: The H27TDG8T2D5R represents SK Hynix's leadership in 3D NAND technology. With 1Tb density in a single die, this NAND enables unprecedented storage capacities in compact form factors. The 1200MT/s interface speed is impressive, providing bandwidth for demanding enterprise workloads. In my experience with data center deployments, this NAND delivers excellent performance in high-capacity SSDs. The reliability characteristics are solid, with 1,000-3,000 P/E cycles supporting long product lifecycles. I particularly recommend this NAND for hyperscale data center applications where storage density and cost per GB are critical. The advanced error correction ensures data integrity in demanding enterprise environments.
faeReview.highlight: 1Tb density enabling unprecedented storage capacities for data center applications

alternativeParts.reason: Better supply availability for high-volume deployments

companionParts.description: Phison E16 NVMe controller for high-speed SSDs
QLC version for maximum capacity storage

faqs.question: What are the benefits of 1Tb NAND for SSD manufacturers?
faqs.answer: 1Tb NAND provides significant benefits for SSD manufacturers: 1) Reduced board space - fewer dies required for target capacity, simplifying PCB design and reducing cost, 2) Lower bill of materials - fewer components and less PCB area reduce overall SSD cost, 3) Higher yield potential - fewer die interconnects improve manufacturing yield, 4) Simplified thermal design - fewer hot spots from fewer die, 5) Supply chain efficiency - reduced part numbers simplify inventory management. For example, a 4TB SSD requires only 32 of the 1Tb dies vs 64 of the 512Gb dies, significantly reducing assembly complexity. The cost savings translate to more competitive SSD pricing.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 1Tb NAND for high-capacity SSD designs to reduce cost and simplify manufacturing. Contact FAE for BOM optimization.

faqs.question: How does 1Tb NAND affect SSD performance?
faqs.answer: 1Tb NAND affects SSD performance in several ways: 1) Parallelism - fewer die means potentially reduced parallelism in lower-end controllers, 2) Die performance - 1Tb dies typically achieve similar per-die performance to 512Gb, 3) Controller optimization - modern controllers are optimized for high-density NAND, 4) Real-world impact - minimal difference for typical consumer workloads, 5) Enterprise impact - enterprise SSDs using multiple channels maintain full performance. High-performance NVMe SSDs with 8-16 channel controllers fully utilize parallelism regardless of die density. The performance difference is negligible in real-world usage compared to the cost and manufacturing benefits.
faqs.decisionGuide: Modern NVMe controllers handle 1Tb NAND efficiently. Performance impact is minimal in real-world applications.

faqs.question: What is the BGA-272 package advantage?
faqs.answer: The BGA-272 package for 1Tb NAND offers: 1) Higher pin count - accommodates increased I/O requirements for 1Tb density, 2) Signal integrity - optimized layout for high-speed Toggle DDR interface up to 1200MT/s, 3) Thermal performance - designed for efficient heat dissipation from high-density die, 4) Manufacturing compatibility - compatible with standard BGA assembly processes, 5) Reliability - robust package design for enterprise applications. The larger package enables better electrical performance for the high-speed interface.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use BGA-272 for high-density 1Tb NAND designs. Ensure PCB layout follows SK Hynix guidelines.

faqs.question: How does this NAND support enterprise applications?
faqs.answer: The H27TDG8T2D5R supports enterprise applications through: 1) High reliability - 1,000-3,000 P/E cycles for long service life, 2) Data integrity - advanced LDPC error correction for robust data protection, 3) Performance - 1200MT/s interface for demanding workloads, 4) Quality - SK Hynix enterprise-grade manufacturing and testing, 5) Endurance - suitable for mixed workloads in data center environments. Enterprise SSDs using this NAND can achieve high capacity with fewer components, improving reliability and reducing cost.
faqs.decisionGuide: Suitable for enterprise SSDs requiring high capacity and reliability. Contact FAE for enterprise qualification support.

faqs.question: What is the typical use case for 1Tb NAND?
faqs.answer: Typical use cases for H27TDG8T2D5R 1Tb NAND include: 1) High-capacity NVMe SSDs - 4TB to 16TB consumer and enterprise drives, 2) Data center storage - high-density storage for cloud infrastructure, 3) Surveillance systems - high-capacity storage for video recording, 4) Content delivery - edge storage for CDN nodes, 5) AI training data - large datasets for machine learning. The 1Tb density enables cost-effective high-capacity storage solutions across these applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Ideal for high-capacity SSD designs. Use in applications requiring maximum storage density.

categories.name: SSD
categories.description: Enterprise and client SSDs with high performance, reliability, and data integrity for diverse storage applications
categories.longDescription: SK Hynix SSD products deliver high-performance, reliable storage solutions for enterprise and client applications. The portfolio includes NVMe SSDs for high-performance computing, SATA SSDs for cost-effective storage, and client SSDs for consumer devices. Key features include high sequential and random performance, advanced error correction and data protection, end-to-end data path protection, power loss protection options, and enterprise-grade reliability with high TBW ratings. As an authorized distributor, we provide SSD selection guidance and technical support.

series.name: NVMe SSD
series.description: High-performance NVMe SSDs with PCIe Gen4 interface for demanding workloads

series.name: SATA SSD
series.description: Cost-effective SATA SSDs for capacity and mainstream applications

selectionGuide.title: SK Hynix SSD Selection Guide

recommendations: Consider TBW ratings for write-intensive workloads

selectionGuideLink.articleTitle: How to Select SK Hynix SSD for Your Application
selectionGuideLink.description: Comprehensive guide for selecting SK Hynix SSD products

faqs.question: What is the difference between NVMe and SATA SSDs?
faqs.answer: NVMe and SATA SSDs differ in interface and performance: 1) Interface - NVMe uses PCIe lanes (typically x4), SATA uses AHCI protocol over SATA interface, 2) Performance - NVMe delivers up to 7GB/s read speeds, SATA maxes at 600MB/s (6Gb/s), 3) Latency - NVMe offers 10x lower latency than SATA, 4) IOPS - NVMe provides 10-100x higher IOPS for random access, 5) Cost - NVMe is more expensive per GB than SATA, 6) Use cases - NVMe for high-performance workloads, SATA for capacity and cost-sensitive applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use NVMe for high-performance applications requiring fast storage. Select SATA for cost-effective capacity storage.

faqs.question: What does TBW mean and how do I calculate SSD endurance?
faqs.answer: TBW (Terabytes Written) indicates SSD endurance: 1) Definition - total bytes that can be written before NAND wearout, 2) Calculation - based on NAND P/E cycles and over-provisioning, 3) DWPD - Drive Writes Per Day = TBW / (365 x years x capacity), 4) Example - 1TB SSD with 600TBW = 0.82 DWPD for 5 years, 5) Consumer vs Enterprise - consumer SSDs typically 100-600 TBW, enterprise SSDs 1-10+ PBW. Select SSD with sufficient TBW for your write workload. For heavy write workloads, consider enterprise SSDs with higher TBW ratings.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate DWPD requirements based on daily write volume. Select SSD with adequate TBW rating for application lifetime.

faqs.question: What is power loss protection (PLP) in enterprise SSDs?
faqs.answer: Power Loss Protection (PLP) ensures data integrity during unexpected power failures: 1) Capacitor backup - onboard capacitors provide power to complete in-flight writes, 2) Data flushing - cached data is written to NAND before power loss, 3) Metadata protection - critical metadata is protected and can be recovered, 4) Firmware recovery - SSD can recover to consistent state after power loss, 5) Enterprise requirement - PLP is essential for enterprise applications with critical data. Consumer SSDs typically lack PLP. Enterprise SSDs like SK Hynix PE8010 include comprehensive PLP. Without PLP, power loss can cause data corruption or SSD failure.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use PLP-enabled SSDs for enterprise applications with critical data. Consumer applications may use standard SSDs without PLP.

faqs.question: How do I optimize SSD performance in my system?
faqs.answer: To optimize SSD performance: 1) Enable AHCI/NVMe mode - ensure BIOS is set to AHCI for SATA or NVMe for PCIe SSDs, 2) Update drivers - use latest storage drivers from OS or manufacturer, 3) Enable TRIM - allows OS to inform SSD which blocks are no longer in use, 4) Leave free space - maintain 10-20% free space for optimal performance and wear leveling, 5) Thermal management - ensure adequate cooling as SSDs throttle when overheating, 6) Alignment - ensure proper partition alignment (4KB boundaries). For enterprise SSDs, monitor SMART data and schedule maintenance. SK Hynix SSDs include optimization features that work automatically with proper system configuration.
faqs.decisionGuide: Configure BIOS properly, enable TRIM, maintain free space, and ensure adequate cooling for optimal SSD performance.

faqs.question: What is the difference between client and enterprise SSDs?
faqs.answer: Client and enterprise SSDs differ in design and capabilities: 1) Endurance - enterprise SSDs have higher TBW ratings (1-10 DWPD), client SSDs lower (0.1-0.5 DWPD), 2) Power loss protection - enterprise SSDs include PLP, client SSDs typically do not, 3) Performance consistency - enterprise SSDs maintain consistent performance under heavy loads, 4) Reliability features - enterprise SSDs have enhanced error correction and data path protection, 5) Warranty - enterprise SSDs offer 5-year warranties, client typically 3-5 years, 6) Cost - enterprise SSDs cost 2-3x more than client SSDs. Use enterprise SSDs for servers and critical applications. Client SSDs are suitable for PCs and non-critical workloads.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use enterprise SSDs for servers and mission-critical applications. Use client SSDs for PCs and cost-sensitive applications.

products.name: Enterprise NVMe SSD 3.84TB
products.shortDescription: SK Hynix PE8010 enterprise NVMe SSD 3.84TB high-performance storage with PCIe Gen4 interface
descriptionParagraphs: The PE8010 is a high-performance enterprise NVMe SSD designed for demanding data center workloads. This 3.84TB SSD delivers up to 6,800MB/s sequential read and 4,000MB/s sequential write speeds using PCIe Gen4 x4 interface. The SSD provides exceptional IOPS performance for random read/write operations.

Built with SK Hynix's advanced 3D NAND and enterprise-grade firmware, the PE8010 offers robust data protection including end-to-end data path protection, power loss protection, and advanced ECC. The SSD is optimized for mixed workloads including database, virtualization, and AI/ML training applications.

The U.2 form factor ensures broad server compatibility while the 3.84TB capacity provides substantial storage for enterprise applications. With 1 DWPD endurance rating and 5-year warranty, this SSD delivers reliable performance for demanding data center environments.

faeReview.author: James Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Enterprise Storage
faeReview.content: The PE8010 represents SK Hynix's entry into the enterprise NVMe SSD market with strong performance credentials. The 6,800MB/s read speed is competitive with leading enterprise SSDs. I particularly appreciate the balanced write performance at 4,000MB/s which is excellent for write-intensive workloads. The 1 DWPD endurance is appropriate for mixed workload applications. In data center deployments, this SSD performs reliably under sustained workloads. The power loss protection is critical for data integrity in enterprise environments. I recommend this SSD for database servers, virtualization hosts, and AI training infrastructure where performance and reliability are essential.
faeReview.highlight: Enterprise NVMe SSD with excellent performance and reliability for data center applications

companionParts.description: Ruler to U.2 adapter for E1.S/E1.L SSDs

faqs.question: What are the power loss protection features of this SSD?
faqs.answer: The PE8010 includes comprehensive power loss protection: 1) Capacitor-backed PLP - capacitors provide power to complete in-flight writes during power loss, 2) Metadata protection - critical metadata is protected and recovered, 3) Firmware recovery - SSD firmware can recover after unexpected power loss, 4) Data integrity - end-to-end data path protection ensures data integrity from host to NAND, 5) SMART monitoring - power loss events are logged for troubleshooting. This protection is essential for enterprise applications where data integrity is critical. The PLP implementation meets enterprise reliability requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: PLP is essential for enterprise applications with critical data. This SSD provides robust power loss protection.

faqs.question: What server platforms support this U.2 SSD?
faqs.answer: The PE8010 U.2 SSD is compatible with standard server platforms: 1) Dell PowerEdge servers (R640, R740, R940 series), 2) HPE ProLiant servers (DL360, DL380, DL580 series), 3) Lenovo ThinkSystem servers (SR630, SR650, SR850 series), 4) Supermicro SuperServer series, 5) Any server with U.2/NVMe bay support. The U.2 interface (also known as SFF-8639) provides broad compatibility with enterprise server platforms. Hot-plug support enables easy installation and replacement. NVMe driver support is included in major operating systems and hypervisors.
faqs.decisionGuide: Verify server has U.2/NVMe bays. Most modern enterprise servers support U.2 SSDs. Contact FAE for compatibility verification.

faqs.question: What is the endurance rating and how long will this SSD last?
faqs.answer: The PE8010 has 1 DWPD (Drive Writes Per Day) endurance: 1) Total writes - 7TB written per day for 5 years = 12.775PB total, 2) Calculation - 3.84TB × 1 DWPD × 365 days × 5 years, 3) Real-world usage - most enterprise workloads write 0.1-0.5 DWPD, 4) Wear leveling - advanced wear leveling extends effective life, 5) Monitoring - SMART attributes track wear status. For typical database workloads, this SSD will last well beyond 5 years. Heavy write workloads like logging may approach the limit.
faqs.decisionGuide: 1 DWPD is suitable for most enterprise workloads. Monitor SMART wear indicators and plan replacement at 80% wear.

faqs.question: How does PCIe Gen4 improve performance over Gen3?
faqs.answer: PCIe Gen4 provides significant performance improvements: 1) Bandwidth - 16GT/s vs 8GT/s per lane (2x increase), 2) Sequential speed - up to 6,800MB/s vs ~3,500MB/s for Gen3, 3) Latency - slightly reduced latency due to improved signaling, 4) Future-proofing - Gen4 is the current standard for new servers, 5) Backward compatibility - Gen4 SSDs work in Gen3 slots at reduced speed. To achieve full Gen4 performance, both SSD and server must support Gen4. The performance difference is significant for bandwidth-intensive workloads.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use Gen4 SSDs in Gen4 servers for maximum performance. Gen4 SSDs work in Gen3 slots at reduced speed.

faqs.question: What is the warranty and support for this enterprise SSD?
faqs.answer: The PE8010 includes comprehensive enterprise warranty: 1) Duration - 5-year warranty coverage, 2) TBW limit - warranty covers up to rated TBW (12.775PB for 3.84TB), 3) Support - technical support through SK Hynix and authorized distributors, 4) Replacement - advance replacement options available for critical applications, 5) SMART monitoring - proactive monitoring for predictive maintenance. Enterprise warranty provides peace of mind for mission-critical deployments. Contact SK Hynix or your distributor for warranty claims.
faqs.decisionGuide: 5-year enterprise warranty provides reliable coverage. Monitor SMART data for proactive maintenance.

products.name: Enterprise NVMe SSD 7.68TB
products.shortDescription: SK Hynix PE8030 enterprise NVMe SSD 7.68TB maximum capacity storage for data-intensive applications
descriptionParagraphs: The PE8030 is SK Hynix's highest capacity enterprise NVMe SSD, offering 7.68TB in the standard U.2 form factor. This SSD delivers up to 6,400MB/s sequential read and 3,800MB/s sequential write speeds using PCIe Gen4 x4 interface. The high capacity enables massive data storage in existing server infrastructure.

Optimized for read-intensive data center workloads, the PE8030 provides excellent performance for content delivery, big data analytics, and data warehousing applications. The advanced 3D NAND with high-density architecture ensures reliable long-term storage. Enterprise-grade features include power loss protection, end-to-end data protection, and advanced ECC.

The 7.68TB capacity reduces the number of drives required for high-capacity storage, simplifying data center management and reducing infrastructure costs. With 1 DWPD endurance and 5-year warranty, this SSD delivers reliable performance for capacity-focused enterprise applications.

faeReview.author: Emily Zhang
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Data Center Solutions
faeReview.content: The PE8030 addresses the growing need for high-capacity storage in data centers. With 7.68TB in a single 2.5-inch drive, servers can achieve massive storage density without special form factors. The performance is well-balanced for read-intensive workloads typical in data warehouse and CDN applications. In my experience, customers appreciate the reduced drive count for simplified management. The 1 DWPD endurance is appropriate for read-heavy applications. The power loss protection provides peace of mind for critical data. I recommend this SSD for hyperscale data centers and enterprise storage deployments requiring maximum capacity.
faeReview.highlight: 7.68TB capacity enables massive storage density in standard server infrastructure

alternativeParts.reason: Alternative with slightly higher read performance

companionParts.description: Adapter for E1.S/E1.L form factor conversion

faqs.question: What is the ideal use case for the 7.68TB capacity SSD?
faqs.answer: The PE8030 7.68TB SSD is ideal for capacity-focused applications: 1) Data warehousing - massive storage for analytics and business intelligence platforms, 2) Content delivery - storing large media files for CDN edge nodes, 3) Video surveillance - high-capacity storage for NVR systems with many camera feeds, 4) Backup storage - primary backup target before archiving, 5) Big data - HDFS and Spark storage for data lakes, 6) Virtual desktop infrastructure - storing VM images. The high capacity reduces the number of drives and simplifies storage management. For write-intensive workloads, consider the lower capacity PE8010 with higher write endurance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use PE8030 for read-intensive capacity applications. Select PE8010 for write-intensive workloads requiring higher endurance.

faqs.question: How does this SSD perform in RAID configurations?
faqs.answer: The PE8030 performs well in RAID configurations: 1) Hardware RAID - compatible with RAID controllers (PERC, SmartRAID, MegaRAID), 2) Software RAID - works with Linux mdraid, Windows Storage Spaces, 3) Performance - RAID 0 doubles performance, RAID 10 provides redundancy with performance, 4) Capacity - RAID 5/6 reduces usable capacity but adds protection, 5) Considerations - NVMe SSDs require proper RAID controller or HBA support for RAID features. For critical data, RAID 10 or RAID 6 provides protection against drive failure. The SSD's high IOPS performs well in RAID configurations. Consider RAID write-back cache for optimal performance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use RAID 10 or RAID 6 for critical data protection. Ensure RAID controller supports NVMe SSDs.

faqs.question: What is the total TBW for this 7.68TB SSD?
faqs.answer: The PE8030 7.68TB has 14TBW (Terabytes Written) total endurance: 1) Calculation - 7.68TB × 1 DWPD × 365 days × 5 years = 14TBW, 2) Daily writes - supports 7.68TB written per day for 5 years, 3) Real-world usage - most capacity workloads write less than 0.3 DWPD, 4) Wear leveling - extends effective life beyond rated TBW, 5) Monitoring - SMART attribute 202 tracks percentage used. For read-intensive workloads, the SSD will last well beyond 5 years. Even moderate write workloads stay well within TBW limits.
faqs.decisionGuide: 14TBW provides excellent endurance for capacity workloads. Monitor SMART wear indicators for proactive maintenance.

faqs.question: How does this SSD compare to SATA SSDs in terms of performance?
faqs.answer: The PE8030 NVMe SSD significantly outperforms SATA SSDs: 1) Sequential read - 6,400MB/s vs ~550MB/s for SATA (12x faster), 2) Sequential write - 3,800MB/s vs ~520MB/s for SATA (7x faster), 3) Random IOPS - 800K IOPS vs ~100K IOPS for SATA (8x higher), 4) Latency - NVMe has much lower latency than SATA AHCI, 5) Use cases - NVMe for performance-critical, SATA for cost-sensitive. The performance difference is dramatic for database, virtualization, and analytics workloads. NVMe is essential for modern data center storage.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use NVMe for performance-critical applications. SATA remains viable for archival and backup storage.

faqs.question: What thermal management is required for this SSD?
faqs.answer: The PE8030 requires proper thermal management: 1) Operating temperature - 0C to 70C ambient, 2) Power consumption - 8W typical, 12W peak, 3) Cooling - server airflow typically adequate, 4) Throttling - SSD reduces performance if overheating, 5) Monitoring - SMART temperature attribute tracks drive temp. Ensure server has adequate airflow through drive bays. High-density storage servers may require additional cooling. Monitor temperatures during stress testing.
faqs.decisionGuide: Standard server airflow is typically sufficient. Monitor temperatures and ensure adequate cooling in dense configurations.

categories.name: HBM
categories.description: High Bandwidth Memory (HBM) for AI, HPC, and graphics applications requiring extreme memory bandwidth
categories.longDescription: SK Hynix HBM (High Bandwidth Memory) delivers extreme memory bandwidth for demanding AI, HPC, and graphics applications. The 3D-stacked memory architecture provides massive bandwidth in a compact form factor. SK Hynix offers HBM2E and HBM3 generations with capacities from 8GB to 24GB and bandwidth up to 1.5TB/s. As an authorized distributor, we provide HBM selection guidance and technical support for AI accelerator and GPU designs.

series.name: HBM2E
series.description: Second-generation HBM with up to 1TB/s bandwidth and 16GB capacity

series.name: HBM3
series.description: Third-generation HBM with up to 1.5TB/s bandwidth and 24GB capacity

selectionGuide.title: SK Hynix HBM Selection Guide
selectionGuide.content: Guide for selecting optimal HBM based on application requirements, bandwidth needs, and capacity
recommendations: Use HBM3 for next-generation AI accelerators

selectionGuideLink.articleTitle: How to Select SK Hynix HBM for Your Application
selectionGuideLink.description: Comprehensive guide for selecting SK Hynix HBM products

faqs.question: What is High Bandwidth Memory (HBM) and why is it important?
faqs.answer: High Bandwidth Memory (HBM) is a 3D-stacked memory technology providing extreme bandwidth: 1) Architecture - memory dies stacked vertically with through-silicon vias (TSV), 2) Bandwidth - up to 1.5TB/s with HBM3, much higher than DDR memory, 3) Efficiency - wide interface (1024-bit) at low voltage achieves high bandwidth with lower power, 4) Applications - AI training, HPC, graphics GPUs, and networking processors. HBM solves the memory bandwidth bottleneck in compute-intensive applications. Without HBM, AI accelerators would be limited by memory bandwidth, not compute capability.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use HBM for applications requiring extreme memory bandwidth. Contact our FAE for system co-design guidance.

faqs.question: What is the difference between HBM2E and HBM3?
faqs.answer: HBM2E and HBM3 differ in performance and capabilities: 1) Bandwidth - HBM2E up to 1TB/s, HBM3 up to 1.5TB/s (50% increase), 2) Capacity - HBM2E up to 16GB, HBM3 up to 24GB per stack, 3) Data rate - HBM2E at 3.2GT/s per pin, HBM3 at 6.4GT/s (2x increase), 4) Power - HBM3 achieves higher bandwidth with improved power efficiency, 5) Applications - HBM2E for current AI accelerators, HBM3 for next-generation AI training and HPC systems. HBM3 is essential for training large language models and advanced AI applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use HBM3 for next-generation AI designs. HBM2E remains viable for current-generation systems.

faqs.question: What are the thermal challenges with HBM integration?
faqs.answer: HBM integration presents unique thermal challenges: 1) High power density - 3D stacking concentrates heat in small area, 2) Limited heat dissipation paths - memory dies stacked above logic die restrict cooling options, 3) Thermal interface - requires high-performance thermal interface material between HBM and heat spreader, 4) System cooling - may require liquid cooling or advanced air cooling for high-power AI accelerators, 5) Thermal throttling - HBM may throttle performance if temperature exceeds limits. Typical HBM operating temperature is up to 105C. Thermal simulation is essential during system design. SK Hynix provides thermal guidelines and simulation support for HBM integration.
faqs.decisionGuide: Plan for advanced thermal solutions when integrating HBM. Conduct thermal simulation early in design cycle.

faqs.question: What is 2.5D packaging and why is it required for HBM?
faqs.answer: 2.5D packaging enables HBM integration with processors: 1) Interposer technology - silicon interposer provides high-density routing between HBM and processor, 2) TSV connections - through-silicon vias connect stacked memory dies, 3) Microbump bonding - fine-pitch connections between dies and interposer, 4) Benefits - enables wide 1024-bit interface in compact footprint, 5) Manufacturing - requires advanced packaging capabilities from foundries like TSMC and Samsung. 2.5D packaging is essential because standard PCB routing cannot support HBM's high-speed, wide-interface requirements. The technology adds cost but enables performance impossible with traditional packaging.
faqs.decisionGuide: 2.5D packaging is required for HBM. Engage packaging partners early and plan for additional cost and complexity.

faqs.question: How do I select between HBM2E and HBM3 for my AI accelerator?
faqs.answer: Selecting between HBM2E and HBM3 depends on your requirements: 1) Performance needs - HBM3 provides 50% more bandwidth (1.5TB/s vs 1TB/s), essential for large model training, 2) Model size - HBM3's 24GB capacity accommodates larger models than HBM2E's 16GB, 3) Power budget - HBM3 is more power-efficient per GB/s, 4) Timeline - HBM2E has mature supply chain, HBM3 is newer with growing availability, 5) Cost - HBM3 commands premium pricing over HBM2E. For next-generation AI training with 100B+ parameter models, HBM3 is recommended. For current-generation designs or inference applications, HBM2E provides excellent value. Contact SK Hynix for roadmap and availability information.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use HBM3 for next-gen large model training. Use HBM2E for current designs and inference applications.

products.name: HBM2E 16GB
products.shortDescription: SK Hynix HBM2E 16GB High Bandwidth Memory for AI accelerators and high-performance computing
descriptionParagraphs: The HBM2E 16GB is SK Hynix's second-generation High Bandwidth Memory solution delivering up to 1TB/s bandwidth. This 16GB memory stack is designed for AI accelerators, GPUs, and high-performance computing applications requiring massive memory bandwidth.

Using 8-layer 3D stacking technology with TSV (Through-Silicon Via) interconnects, the HBM2E provides 1024-bit wide interface operating at 3.2GT/s per pin. The memory architecture enables AI training accelerators to access large neural network weights without memory bandwidth bottlenecks.

The HBM2E is widely adopted in AI training accelerators from major AI chip companies. The proven technology and broad ecosystem support make it ideal for AI accelerator designs. With 1TB/s bandwidth and 16GB capacity, it provides the memory resources needed for modern AI workloads.

faeReview.author: Kevin Liu
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - AI Memory Solutions
faeReview.content: The HBM2E 16GB has become the de facto standard for AI training accelerators. In my experience supporting AI chip companies, this memory provides the bandwidth required for training large neural networks. The 1TB/s bandwidth eliminates memory bottlenecks that would otherwise limit AI accelerator utilization. The 16GB capacity accommodates modern AI models with billions of parameters. SK Hynix has proven manufacturing capability ensuring supply for high-volume AI deployments. I recommend HBM2E for AI accelerator designs targeting current-generation AI training applications.
faeReview.highlight: Industry-standard HBM2E providing 1TB/s bandwidth for AI training workloads

alternativeParts.useCase: Edge AI accelerators with lower memory needs

companionParts.description: AI accelerator chip requiring HBM memory interface
HBM3 version for higher performance designs

faqs.question: What is required to integrate HBM2E into an AI accelerator design?
faqs.answer: HBM2E integration requires careful system design: 1) AI accelerator ASIC - must include HBM memory controller and PHY, 2) 2.5D packaging - HBM requires interposer or substrate interposer technology, 3) Signal integrity - high-speed HBM interfaces require careful PCB and interposer design, 4) Thermal management - HBM stacks generate significant heat requiring thermal solution, 5) Co-design - memory and accelerator are tightly coupled requiring joint optimization. The HBM2E interface follows JEDEC standards, but successful integration requires close cooperation between AI chip designer, packaging partner, and memory supplier. SK Hynix provides system co-design support for AI accelerator customers.
faqs.decisionGuide: HBM integration requires system co-design. Contact SK Hynix early in design for co-design support.

faqs.question: What AI accelerators use HBM2E memory?
faqs.answer: HBM2E is used in many AI accelerators: 1) NVIDIA A100 and H100 GPUs - use HBM2E/HBM3 for AI training, 2) AMD CDNA GPUs - employ HBM2E for AI workloads, 3) Google TPU v4 - uses custom HBM2E-like memory, 4) Various AI startup accelerators - includingGraphcore, Cerebras, and SambaNova systems. The AI industry has standardized on HBM for training workloads due to the massive bandwidth requirements. HBM2E provides the right balance of bandwidth, capacity, and manufacturing maturity for current AI accelerator designs.
faqs.decisionGuide: HBM2E is the standard for AI training accelerators. Consider HBM3 for next-generation designs.

faqs.question: What is the power consumption of HBM2E?
faqs.answer: HBM2E power consumption characteristics: 1) Active power - approximately 1.2W per GB under load, 2) Idle power - significantly lower when not actively accessed, 3) Total power - 16GB HBM2E consumes approximately 15-20W in active AI workloads, 4) Power efficiency - much better than GDDR for bandwidth provided, 5) Thermal design - requires heat spreader and adequate cooling. The 1.2V operating voltage provides good power efficiency for the extreme bandwidth. System designers must account for HBM power in overall thermal budget.
faqs.decisionGuide: Plan for 15-20W power consumption in thermal design. HBM2E provides excellent power efficiency per GB/s.

faqs.question: What is the typical development timeline for HBM integration?
faqs.answer: HBM integration typically requires 12-18 months: 1) Architecture phase (3 months) - define memory requirements and system architecture, 2) Co-design phase (6 months) - work with SK Hynix on interface design and interposer planning, 3) Implementation phase (6 months) - ASIC design, packaging design, and layout, 4) Validation phase (3 months) - silicon bring-up, characterization, and system validation. Start engagement with SK Hynix at architecture phase. Packaging partner selection is critical and should happen early.
faqs.decisionGuide: Plan 12-18 month timeline for HBM integration. Engage SK Hynix early in architecture phase.

faqs.question: How does HBM2E compare to GDDR6 for AI applications?
faqs.answer: HBM2E vs GDDR6 comparison for AI: 1) Bandwidth - HBM2E provides 1TB/s vs GDDR6's 64-128GB/s per chip, 2) Power efficiency - HBM2E is 3-4x more power efficient per GB/s, 3) Form factor - HBM2E compact 2.5D vs GDDR6 discrete packages, 4) Capacity - HBM2E 16GB per stack vs GDDR6 16-32GB total with multiple chips, 5) Cost - HBM2E higher cost but justified by performance. For AI training, HBM2E is the clear winner due to bandwidth requirements. GDDR6 may be suitable for inference with lower bandwidth needs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use HBM2E for AI training requiring maximum bandwidth. GDDR6 may be suitable for inference applications.

products.name: HBM3 24GB
products.shortDescription: SK Hynix HBM3 24GB next-generation High Bandwidth Memory with 1.5TB/s bandwidth for advanced AI
descriptionParagraphs: The HBM3 24GB is SK Hynix's third-generation High Bandwidth Memory delivering up to 1.5TB/s bandwidth. This 24GB memory stack represents the cutting edge of AI memory technology, providing 50% more bandwidth and 50% more capacity than HBM2E.

Featuring 12-layer 3D stacking with advanced TSV technology, the HBM3 achieves data rates of 6.4GT/s per pin, doubling the speed of HBM2E. The 24GB capacity provides ample memory for large language models and advanced AI networks with billions of parameters.

HBM3 is essential for next-generation AI training systems including large language model training, autonomous driving AI, and advanced scientific simulations. The memory's massive bandwidth enables AI accelerators to achieve higher utilization by eliminating memory bottlenecks.

faeReview.author: Dr. Amanda Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Advanced AI Memory
faeReview.content: HBM3 represents a major leap in AI memory technology. The 1.5TB/s bandwidth is essential for training the latest large language models with hundreds of billions of parameters. In my experience supporting AI system designers, HBM3 enables new AI architectures that were previously memory-bound. The 24GB capacity accommodates the massive weight matrices in modern neural networks. The improved power efficiency at 1.1V helps manage thermal constraints in high-density AI systems. HBM3 is the memory technology that will enable the next wave of AI breakthroughs.
faeReview.highlight: 1.5TB/s bandwidth enabling next-generation large language model training

companionParts.description: Next-generation AI accelerator with HBM3 interface

faqs.question: What are the key differences between HBM3 and HBM2E packaging?
faqs.answer: HBM3 and HBM2E have similar packaging approaches but different requirements: 1) Thermal constraints - HBM3's higher power density requires more robust thermal solutions, 2) Signal integrity - higher data rates in HBM3 demand tighter signal integrity design, 3) Interposer design - HBM3 may require larger interposers with more routing layers, 4) Test challenges - HBM3's higher speed requires advanced test equipment, 5) Co-design - both require close system co-design, HBM3 more so. Despite challenges, HBM3 uses similar 2.5D packaging principles. The ecosystem for HBM3 is maturing rapidly with TSMC and Samsung providing advanced packaging options.
faqs.decisionGuide: HBM3 requires advanced packaging capabilities. Engage packaging partners early in design.

faqs.question: What AI applications specifically benefit from HBM3?
faqs.answer: HBM3 benefits applications requiring extreme memory bandwidth and capacity: 1) Large language model training - GPT-4, PaLM, and similar models with 100B+ parameters, 2) Autonomous driving - processing sensor data from cameras, LiDAR, radar in real-time, 3) Scientific simulation - molecular dynamics, climate modeling, materials science, 4) Recommendation systems - training with massive user-item interaction data, 5) Protein folding - AlphaFold-style computations requiring large memory footprint. HBM3 enables AI systems that were previously impossible due to memory limitations. The 1.5TB/s bandwidth and 24GB capacity unlock new possibilities in AI research and deployment.
faqs.decisionGuide: HBM3 is essential for large language models and advanced AI research. Contact our team for HBM3 system design support.

faqs.question: What is the power consumption improvement of HBM3 over HBM2E?
faqs.answer: HBM3 provides significant power efficiency improvements: 1) Voltage reduction - HBM3 operates at 1.1V vs HBM2E's 1.2V, 2) Power per GB/s - approximately 20% more efficient than HBM2E, 3) Total power - 24GB HBM3 consumes similar power to 16GB HBM2E despite higher capacity, 4) Thermal benefits - lower power reduces cooling requirements, 5) System impact - enables higher compute density in AI accelerators. The improved power efficiency is crucial for managing thermal constraints in high-performance AI systems.
faqs.decisionGuide: HBM3 provides better power efficiency. Plan thermal design for similar power as HBM2E despite higher capacity.

faqs.question: What is the availability timeline for HBM3?
faqs.answer: HBM3 availability timeline: 1) Current status - HBM3 is in production and shipping to major AI chip companies, 2) Supply ramp - production volume increasing throughout 2024-2025, 3) Lead times - currently longer than HBM2E due to high demand, 4) Roadmap - SK Hynix continues to expand HBM3 capacity, 5) Next-gen - HBM3E and HBM4 in development. For new AI accelerator designs, HBM3 is the recommended choice for production starting in 2024. Contact SK Hynix for specific availability and allocation.
faqs.decisionGuide: HBM3 is available for production designs. Plan for potential lead times due to high demand.

faqs.question: How does HBM3 support large language model training?
faqs.answer: HBM3 enables large language model training through: 1) Massive bandwidth - 1.5TB/s keeps GPU/TPU compute units fed with data, 2) Large capacity - 24GB accommodates billion-parameter model weights, 3) Model parallelism - multiple HBM3 stacks enable trillion-parameter models, 4) Training efficiency - high bandwidth reduces training time significantly, 5) Scalability - HBM3 supports scaling to hundreds of accelerators. GPT-4 class models with 100B+ parameters require HBM3's combination of bandwidth and capacity. Without HBM3, these models would be memory-bound.
faqs.decisionGuide: HBM3 is essential for training 100B+ parameter models. Contact SK Hynix for LLM system design support.
